pull.title: Make theStack a maintainer and a security contact
pull.body: @sipa, @jonasnick and I [proposed in a recent Bitcoin Core dev meeting](https://achow101.com/ircmeetings/2026/bitcoin-core-dev.2026-06-25_16_00.log.html#f6c02cf2aae64685af41255b948d8eb5) to add @theStack as a maintainer of libsecp256k1. The purpose of this PR is to seek (N)ACKs for the maintainer change and for adding his public key fingerprint to SECURITY.md.

repo.name: secp256k1
repo.description: Optimized C library for EC operations on curve secp256k1

repo.name: secp256k1
repo.description: Optimized C library for EC operations on curve secp256k1

rename.from: Make theStack a maintainer and make him a security contact
rename.to: Make theStack a maintainer and a security contact

events.body:
There seems to be no available key in `keys.openpgp.org` nor `keyserver.ubuntu.com`. It would be nice to upload it there.

I was able to verify it against the one in the guix repo: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bitcoin-core/guix.sigs/main/builder-keys/theStack.gpg
ACK d5c64ba. matches the one in [Stack's gist](https://gist.github.com/theStack/60ed7ccc7c0caffeef2d001b8b54148b).

ACK d5c64bafc7f1d9bc0cfd857840d3014ffaedb7a3

> There seems to be no available key in keys.openpgp.org nor keyserver.ubuntu.com.

The instructions in SECURITY.md worked for me:
```
❯ gpg --keyserver hkps://keys.openpgp.org --recv-keys "6A8F 9C26 6528 E25A EB1D  7731 C237 1D91 CB71 6EA7"
gpg: key 0xC2371D91CB716EA7: public key "Sebastian Falbesoner (theStack) <sebastian.falbesoner@gmail.com>" imported
gpg: Total number processed: 1
gpg:               imported: 1
```
